content.observe: Not every transfer needs the CPU standing over it. Hand the disk controller a source, a destination, and a length — it moves the data itself.
content.fault: The CPU tried to babysit a large transfer and stalled everything behind it. It should have delegated.

hotspots.label: CPU
hotspots.detail: Issues the DMA request, then is free to execute other instructions while the transfer runs.

hotspots.label: DMA Controller
hotspots.detail: Takes over the bus and moves data directly between memory and a device.

hotspots.label: Main Memory
hotspots.detail: Source or destination of the transfer, written to directly by the DMA controller.

hotspots.label: I/O Device
hotspots.detail: The disk, network card, or other device sending or receiving the block.
